question: In what order will callbacks execute: setTimeout(fn, 0), process.nextTick(fn), Promise.resolve().then(fn)?

options.text: nextTick → Promise → setTimeout
setTimeout → nextTick → Promise
options.explanation: setTimeout is a macrotask, it executes after all microtasks. nextTick and Promise are microtasks, but nextTick has higher priority

options.text: Promise → nextTick → setTimeout
options.explanation: process.nextTick has its own queue with higher priority than the Promise microtask queue

options.text: The order is undefined
options.explanation: The order is well-defined: nextTick queue drains first, then Promise queue, then macrotasks

question: What happens if you recursively call process.nextTick?

options.text: The Event Loop is blocked — I/O and timers won't execute
Node.js automatically limits recursion depth
options.explanation: Modern Node.js has no nextTick depth limit. The --max-tick-depth flag was removed in v0.12

options.text: Callbacks will execute in parallel in the thread pool
options.explanation: nextTick callbacks run in the main thread, not the thread pool. Node.js is single-threaded for JavaScript

options.text: Node.js throws a Maximum call stack size exceeded error
options.explanation: nextTick doesn't cause stack recursion — each callback runs separately. The problem is the queue grows infinitely

question: What is the order of setTimeout(fn, 0) and setImmediate(fn) inside an fs.readFile callback?

options.text: setImmediate always first, setTimeout second
setTimeout always first, setImmediate second
options.explanation: Inside an I/O callback (Poll phase), the next phase is Check (setImmediate), not Timers

options.text: The order is undefined
options.explanation: In the main module the order is undefined, but inside I/O callbacks: always setImmediate → setTimeout

options.text: They execute simultaneously
options.explanation: JavaScript is single-threaded — two callbacks cannot execute simultaneously

question: Which statements about the Poll phase are true?

options.text: It processes I/O operation callbacks (fs.readFile, net)
It can block waiting for new I/O events
It executes setTimeout callbacks
options.explanation: setTimeout executes in the Timers phase, not Poll

options.text: When setImmediate is present, it moves to Check phase

question: Why does setTimeout(fn, 0) actually have a delay of >= 1ms?

options.text: Node.js sets a minimum delay of 1ms for setTimeout
The V8 engine doesn't support zero delay
options.explanation: V8 doesn't manage timers — libuv does. The 1ms minimum is a Node.js architectural decision

options.text: The operating system doesn't allow delays under 1ms
options.explanation: The OS can support more precise timers. The 1ms limit is a Node.js/libuv decision

options.text: This is a bug in Node.js that they plan to fix
options.explanation: This is intentional behavior. The HTML spec mandates 4ms minimum (after 5 nested calls), Node.js uses 1ms
